chef_bio.background: A tiny counter soba shop that has operated for over 47 years in the heart of Nagano's Gondo arcade, tucked into the entrance of the historic Nagano Shochiku Aioiza cinema — a Showa-era neighborhood landmark. The proprietor, an elderly man who runs the shop alone, relocated from Togakushi — Shinshu's most revered soba village — to bring hand-cut noodles to the city.
chef_bio.philosophy: The owner describes his approach simply: 'No particular philosophy, but I work diligently every day to make delicious soba. ' The narrow counter creates a quiet intimacy — regulars, salarymen, and film-goers sharing the same eleven stools.
chef_bio.specialty: Tororo soba: Togakushi-style soba arranged in botchi-mori (five rounded portions on a bamboo mat), served with grated mountain yam topped with an egg yolk, and a sharp katsuobushi-based dipping broth the diner adjusts with soba yu

anecdotes.text: Celebrity autographs line the walls, evidence of a long-running local reputation that extends well beyond the arcade neighborhood.
The menu includes 大まくら (Omakura), a double-portion zaru soba with nine bundles — a quiet indulgence for serious soba eaters.
Soba yu (the hot cooking water) is served at the end of the meal and noted for its gentle, clean flavor — a Togakushi tradition.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Udon (100% wheat) is prepared and served in the same 11-seat counter kitchen alongside soba. Cross-contamination with wheat flour is highly probable. No separation noted in any source.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Dipping tsuyu is described as sharp and soy-forward, consistent with standard wheat-containing soy sauce. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternative is mentioned in any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All broth is katsuobushi (bonito) based. No kombu-only or vegan dashi option is noted. Animal-stock contact is unavoidable.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Solo elderly owner; no allergy handling, allergen menu, or dietary accommodation noted in any source. No English communication indicated. Contact at 026-234-2670 if needed.

positives.text: Buckwheat soba itself is a naturally gluten-free grain; the noodles are hand-cut by the owner from Togakushi-sourced flour — no industrial additives noted.

cultural_comfort.note: Counter-only, elderly solo owner, no English signage or menu noted — comfortable for Japanese speakers; non-Japanese diners can point at the menu but communication will be in Japanese.

places.name: SOBA＆DINER 戸隠つきや 門前 (Soba & Diner Togakure Tsukiya Monzen)
places.gf_detail: Standard Togakure soba in Japan typically uses a blend of buckwheat and wheat flour (つなぎ) unless explicitly labeled 十割 (100% buckwheat). This restaurant's soba type (buckwheat-to-wheat ratio) is not documented on any fetched source. Tsuyu (dipping sauce) contains soy sauce, which in Japan standardly contains wheat. Tempura is on the menu, representing a significant cross-contamination risk. No gluten-free menu, dedicated preparation area, or allergy protocol has been documented. Guests with celiac disease must inquire directly (Japanese only).
places.vegan_detail: Dashi broth is fish-based (鰹節 — katsuobushi), confirmed by visitor reports describing the distinctive dried-fish dashi aroma. No vegan dashi alternative is documented. Side dishes include kamo-negi soba (duck) and horse meat yukhoe. Hidden animal ingredients in the broth make this unsafe for vegans without direct kitchen negotiation.
places.hours_raw: Wed–Sun 11:30–14:30, 17:00–20:30 (last order 20:00); closed Mon & Tue
places.cuisine: Togakushi soba, à la carte dishes, local sake
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Opened August 2, 2024 as the Nagano sister location of a Tokyo izakaya-soba concept. The owners have roots in the Togakure area of Nagano and previously ran an izakaya in Nagano City before establishing a Nishi-Azabu outpost in Tokyo.

chef_bio.background: Opened August 2, 2024 as the Nagano sister location of a Tokyo izakaya-soba concept. The owners have roots in the Togakure area of Nagano and previously ran an izakaya in Nagano City before establishing a Nishi-Azabu outpost in Tokyo.
chef_bio.philosophy: Seasonal and local: the kitchen uses wild mountain vegetables the owner gathers personally in spring, and foraged mushrooms in autumn. The result is a soba-izakaya that pairs aromatic buckwheat noodles with carefully chosen Shinshu sake.
chef_bio.specialty: 戸隠霧下そば（Togakure kiri-shita soba）— buckwheat grown at high altitude in the Togakure highlands where temperature swings and persistent fog concentrate flavor. Served in the traditional ぼっち盛り style: five compact bundles arranged on a bamboo sieve, echoing the five deities of the Ama-no-Iwato legend tied to Togakure.

anecdotes.text: The soba dipping broth at つきや is notably different from the sweeter Nagano norm — reviewers describe a pronounced katsuobushi aroma that leans toward a Tokyo-style tsuyu, reflecting the owners' years running a soba izakaya in Nishi-Azabu.

The dining room fuses Showa retro warmth with a café-bar aesthetic — counter seats make solo diners comfortable, and daytime drinking is welcomed, making it unusual among soba shops in the monzen district.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tempura is a menu staple here (かき揚げ丼, 天丼, 天ぷら盛). Fryer oil is almost certainly shared; no dedicated GF fryer is documented.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Tsuyu (dipping broth) and likely all cooking sauces use standard Japanese soy sauce, which contains wheat. No wheat-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned on any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish-based dashi (katsuobushi) is used in the tsuyu and broth. Duck (鴨ねぎ) and horse meat (ユッケ) appear on the menu. No plant-based dashi alternative documented.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy menu, allergy questionnaire, or documented staff allergy protocol found across any source. Phone reservations are accepted (026-233-8028); celiac or vegan diners should call ahead in Japanese to discuss needs.

positives.text: Buckwheat (soba) is naturally gluten-free in its pure form. If the shop uses 十割そば (100% buckwheat, no wheat binder), the noodles themselves could be GF — but this has not been confirmed for this restaurant.

cultural_comfort.note: No English menu or signage documented; staff communication is expected to be in Japanese only.

places.name: 日本料理 旬花 (Nihon Ryori Shunka)
places.gf_detail: Traditional kaiseki cuisine routinely uses wheat-containing soy sauce (shoyu) in marinades, sauces, and simmered dishes. Dashi is typically made with bonito (katsuo) and/or kombu; flour-based preparations may appear in steamed dishes. No gluten-free menu or dedicated allergen protocol is published on the official site or in any review. Because all dining is reservation-only, guests with celiac disease or wheat allergy should contact the restaurant in advance (026-233-7600) to discuss whether substitutions are possible — there is no indication this has been done before.
places.vegan_detail: Kaiseki courses are built around seasonal fish, seafood, and Nagano branded meats; dashi is bonito-based. No vegan or plant-based menu exists. Shinshu traditional vegetables are featured as accompaniments, not as the core. Reservation-only format may allow advance requests for plant-based adjustments, but no evidence of this has been published.
places.hours_raw: Lunch 11:30–15:00, dinner 17:30–22:30; reservations required for both sessions; irregular days off
places.neighborhood: Zenkoji approach / Daimon-cho (東町, Patio Daimon complex)
places.notes: Chef Nakamura was born in Nagano Prefecture in 1976 and trained at Japanese restaurants across Nagano and Tokyo before returning to his home prefecture. He opened Shunka in 2005 and relocated it to the historic Yokikan building within the Patio Daimon complex in 2014, simultaneou

chef_bio.background: Chef Nakamura was born in Nagano Prefecture in 1976 and trained at Japanese restaurants across Nagano and Tokyo before returning to his home prefecture. He opened Shunka in 2005 and relocated it to the historic Yokikan building within the Patio Daimon complex in 2014, simultaneously opening the sister restaurant Tempura Kakure Shunka on the first floor.
chef_bio.philosophy: Nakamura's guiding principle is to convey 'the everlasting taste of Shinshu through kaiseki as the seasons change. ' He has reinvented the conventional kaiseki format — replacing many small bites with fewer generous courses — and has studied the tea ceremony to deepen his understanding of Japanese hospitality and aesthetics.
chef_bio.specialty: Seasonal kaiseki courses featuring Shinshu heirloom vegetables and Nagano branded meats; the chef's signature approach reduces the traditional proliferation of small kaiseki courses in favour of fewer, more substantial and impactful dishes

anecdotes.text: When the original Shunka location suffered fire damage, Nakamura moved operations to an adjacent annex on the second floor of the Meiji-era Yokikan pavilion — a 150-year-old former reception hall within Patio Daimon — which has since become the permanent and atmospheric home of the restaurant.

Nakamura offers a dish he calls 'Chef's Ohagi' — rice cooked in dashi made from local Kijima mochi rice blended with Koshihikari — as a reflection of his belief that even the most humble preparation deserves technique and regional identity.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information about cross-contamination controls has been published. The kitchen prepares traditional kaiseki where wheat-based soy sauce is a foundational ingredient; the risk of cross-contact for celiac diners is unquantified.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (shoyu), which contains wheat, is a core ingredient in kaiseki cooking — used in marinades, simmered preparations (nimono), and dipping sauces. No wheat-free tamari or alternative is mentioned anywhere on the official site or in any review.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Bonito-based dashi (katsuo dashi) is the expected stock base for kaiseki cuisine. No vegan dashi alternative is mentioned. Seafood, fish, poultry, and beef all appear across the seasonal menus.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The Hitosara listing notes that 'allergen accommodations are available upon request,' but no protocol or specific substitutions are described. The reservation-only format does allow for advance dietary communication by phone.

positives.text: All dining is by advance reservation only, creating a natural window for guests to communicate dietary needs before arrival — an advantage over walk-in venues.
English menus and reportedly some English-speaking staff are available, reducing communication barriers for foreign diners with dietary needs.

cultural_comfort.note: Reservation-only kaiseki with private tatami rooms and formal Japanese hospitality; English menus and some English-speaking staff are reportedly available, but the experience is rooted in traditional Japanese fine-dining etiquette.

places.name: ピナティ (Pinatis)
places.gf_detail: This is a wheat bakery. All breads are made with Nagano-grown wheat flour, whole wheat, and rye. No gluten-free products are mentioned on any source. Cross-contamination from wheat flour is unavoidable throughout the kitchen.
places.vegan_detail: Vegan croissants (バター・牛乳・卵不使用) are a confirmed menu item. One source indicates core hard breads also use no dairy or eggs. However the bakery uses wheat flour throughout — this is not a vegan-dedicated shop and the menu changes daily/seasonally. Always confirm on the day.
places.hours_raw: Thu, Fri, Sat 9:30–17:30 (closes once sold out), closed Mon, Tue, Wed & Sun
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Pinatis was opened in June 2019 by a baker who relocated from Tokyo to Nagano's historic Zenko-ji gate district. The owner personally searched the neighborhood on foot for the right property, settling on a renovated old house in the quiet residential enclave of Hanasaki-cho. The

chef_bio.background: Pinatis was opened in June 2019 by a baker who relocated from Tokyo to Nagano's historic Zenko-ji gate district. The owner personally searched the neighborhood on foot for the right property, settling on a renovated old house in the quiet residential enclave of Hanasaki-cho. The shop runs just three days a week — Thursday through Saturday — with the baker working through the night listening to the radio to have fresh loaves ready by 9:30 AM.
chef_bio.philosophy: No added sugar or oil in the core breads; ingredient-forward simplicity. The yeast culture rotates with the seasons, giving the bread a subtly different character throughout the year. Additives are avoided entirely.
chef_bio.specialty: Hard-style sourdough loaves — particularly pain de campagne — made with Nagano-grown wheat, whole wheat, and rye flours, leavened with wild yeast cultivated from seasonal local fruits (kinkan, strawberries from the shop's own garden, Iizuna apples, persimmons, prunes).

anecdotes.text: The baker finds bread-making meditative — describing the late-night baking sessions before opening day as half relaxation, working by radio light while the dough ferments.
The vegan croissant — made without butter, milk, or eggs — appeared on the menu as a permanent item, an unusual offering for a hard-bread-focused sourdough bakery.

gf_cross_contamination.text: This is an active wheat bakery. All products are made with wheat, whole wheat, and rye flours. Flour dust and cross-contamination are inherent to the environment. Not suitable for celiac disease or serious gluten intolerance.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The kitchen handles dairy and egg ingredients (standard croissants alongside vegan croissants). Cross-contact risk for strict vegans cannot be ruled out without direct confirmation from the baker.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling protocols are documented in any source. The shop is a very small one-person operation; contact via phone (026-405-4769), Instagram, or LINE before visiting if allergies are a concern.

positives.text: Core breads contain no added sugar, oil, or additives. Vegan croissants (no butter, milk, or eggs) are a confirmed menu item.
All ingredients are domestic; no artificial additives or preservatives used.

cultural_comfort.note: A quiet residential-area bakery with no apparent English signage or staff; pointing and basic Japanese numbers will get you through, but no English menu or guidance should be expected.

places.name: CocoChouChou (ココシュシュ)
places.gf_detail: A fully dedicated vegan & gluten-free confectionery workshop where wheat flour is never used on the premises. The venue states 「小麦粉は不使用。米粉やアーモンドの粉などを使います」 (no wheat flour is used; rice flour and almond flour are used instead) and describes itself as 「卵・乳製品・小麦・白砂糖不使用、ヴィーガン＆グルテンフリーのお菓子」 (sweets made without egg, dairy, wheat, or white sugar). Because the entire product line and kitchen are wheat-free, structural cross-contact risk is very low, though the shop does not advertise formal celiac certification or lab testing, so confirming with staff is still wise for a highly sensitive celiac.
places.vegan_detail: 100% plant-based. The brand's own tagline is 「ヴィーガン＆グルテンフリーのおいしい手作りお菓子屋さん」 and every product is made 「卵・乳製品・小麦・白砂糖不使用」 (no egg, dairy, wheat, or white sugar). Creams and chocolates are built on cashew nut, coconut oil, and house-made cashew-nut milk, with additives avoided (「着色料、増粘剤、保存料などの添加物はできる限り使いません」).
places.hours_raw: 11:00–17:00; closed Mon & Tue (check Instagram highlights for event/irregular closures)
places.neighborhood: 北石堂町 (Kita-Ishidō-chō) — approx. 4–6 min walk from Nagano Stn, Zenkoji (善光寺口) exit
places.cuisine: Vegan & gluten-free sweets / patisserie (raw cakes, butter sandwiches, raw chocolate, cookies)

roles: 代表取締役 (Representative Director, 株式会社CocoChouChou)
chef_bio.background: Before founding CocoChouChou, Iida worked in product development for a Tokyo café, where researching veganism and food allergies inspired her to start making and selling sweets free of the three major allergens. She opened the business in February 2017, initially built a following through mail-order, then opened a physical workshop/shop in Miwa, Nagano. The company was incorporated in October 2019, and on 15 January 2022 she relocated to the current café-equipped location near Nagano Station.
chef_bio.philosophy: Body-friendly, additive-free sweets made from natural ingredients without egg, dairy, wheat, or white sugar — allergy-conscious confectionery that is delicious for everyone.
chef_bio.specialty: Shinshu vegan butter sandwiches (巨峰 grape / Shine Muscat), vegan raw cakes (ローケーキ), and vegan raw chocolate (生チョコレート) built on cashew-nut milk.

anecdotes.text: 「東京でカフェの商品開発を手がけていたときに、ヴィーガンや食物アレルギーについて研究したことがきっかけで、3大アレルゲン不使用のスイーツの製造販売をスタート」した。
通信販売を続ける中で「工房でも販売してほしい」という声に応えて長野市三輪に実店舗を開き、2022年に長野駅前のカフェ併設店へ移転オープンした。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式「会社概要」に代表者「飯田 紗央里」、2017年2月開業・2019年10月法人化と明記。Web-Komachi の紹介記事に東京カフェでの商品開発経験とヴィーガン／食物アレルギー研究がきっかけという経歴、長野市三輪から長野駅前への移転経緯が記載されている。出身地など一部は不明。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 小麦粉は完全不使用で、米粉やアーモンドの粉などを使用。工房全体がグルテンフリー専用のため小麦の混入リスクは構造的に極めて低い。
「卵・乳製品・小麦・白砂糖不使用、ヴィーガン＆グルテンフリーのお菓子」と全商品を明記。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: スイーツ専門店のため醤油・味噌など小麦を含む発酵調味料は使用しない。該当なし。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 工房全体が完全ヴィーガンで卵・乳製品を一切扱わないため、動物性食材との交差接触リスクはない。

staff_allergy_handling.text: 店主はカフェ商品開発時に食物アレルギーを研究し、3大アレルゲン不使用スイーツの製造販売から創業。アレルギー対応が事業の中核。

positives.text: 小麦を一切扱わない完全グルテンフリー＆ヴィーガンの菓子工房で、全商品が対象。添加物も「できる限り使いません」。
全国にファンを持つ人気店で通販実績も豊富。長野駅善光寺口から徒歩数分とアクセス良好。

places.name: DOON Shokudo Indosan (DOON食堂 印度山)
places.gf_detail: A tiny North-Indian home-cooking eatery (「日本一小さなインド家庭料理」) in the Hashigo-Yokocho lane off Nawate. The shop's own claim is 「全てのカレーがグルテンフリー・ナッツフリー・シュガーフリー・化学調味料不使用」 (all curries are gluten-free, nut-free, sugar-free and use no MSG). Curries are served with rice (Azumino Koshihikari standard; basmati +¥150), so a celiac orders curry + rice and avoids any bread; standard soy sauce isn't central to Indian curry but confirm no wheat-thickened items. It is a shared home kitchen, so keep to the curries and rice and confirm no cross-contact — solid 'options' and a good kid-friendly curry-and-rice choice.
places.vegan_detail: The bean curry (豆のカレー) is vegan-compatible and, with rice, makes a full vegan + GF meal. Chicken curry uses halal chicken; a vegetarian thali is also offered. Confirm ghee/dairy in specific dishes if strictly vegan.
places.hours_raw: 11:30–19:00 (some sources to 20:00), until sold out; closed Sundays
places.neighborhood: 大手4丁目・うら町はしご横丁（縄手近く）(Ote 4, Hashigo-Yokocho, near Nawate)
places.cuisine: North Indian home-cooking curry (all curries GF)
places.notes: [GF] Every curry is gluten-free, nut-free, sugar-free and MSG-free by the shop's own statement, served with rice — an easy, comforting curry-and-rice meal for a celiac 13-year-old. Order curry over rice (basmati available), skip any bread, and confirm no wheat-thickened item. Tiny counter that closes when sold out, so go early; closed Sundays. Halal chicken; vegan bean curry available.

chef_bio.background: DOON食堂 印度山 is a very small North-Indian home-cooking eatery in Matsumoto's Hashigo-Yokocho lane (大手4-6-18), styling itself 「日本一小さなインド家庭料理」 (Japan's smallest Indian home-cooking restaurant). Its identity centres on honest home-style curries free of gluten, nuts, refined sugar and MSG, served with Azumino Koshihikari or basmati rice. In 2018 it topped the curry category of TripAdvisor's 'Japanese restaurants popular with foreigners'. No named owner-chef biography is documented in the Japanese sources reviewed, so the profile is kept light.
chef_bio.philosophy: Simple, additive-free North-Indian home cooking — all curries gluten-, nut-, sugar- and MSG-free.
chef_bio.specialty: Chicken / keema / bean curries and thali sets with rice

anecdotes.text: Won 1st place in the curry category of TripAdvisor's 2018 'Japanese restaurants popular with foreigners'.
Bills itself as 'Japan's smallest Indian home-cooking restaurant' — a tiny counter that closes once dishes sell out.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: The shop's official site/about page, Web-Komachi, deli-koma, gnavi, Tabelog and visitmatsumoto describe a tiny North-Indian home-cooking curry shop in Ote/Hashigo-Yokocho whose curries are all gluten-, nut-, sugar- and MSG-free, with rice sets and halal chicken; corroborated by the gf-akane blogger. Owner name not documented → low-ish confidence.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Shop states all curries are gluten-free; served with rice (no naan on menu). Shared home kitchen — confirm no wheat-thickened item and clean serving.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: North Indian curries; soy sauce not typical, but confirm no wheat/hing-with-wheat in any dish.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Bean curry is vegan-compatible; chicken curry uses halal chicken — confirm ghee/dairy separation for strict vegans.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Own statement: all curries gluten-free, nut-free, sugar-free, no MSG; featured on a wheat-allergy blogger's Matsumoto GF tour.

positives.text: Every curry is gluten-, nut-, sugar- and MSG-free by the shop's own claim, with rice — a reliable curry-and-rice option.

places.name: Enishi (縁-enishi- sorghum&glutenfree)
places.gf_detail: A dedicated gluten-free specialty bakery using Shinshu-grown sorghum (ソルガム／タカキビ) and Kijimadaira rice flour. The shop states its products are made in a workshop that never brings wheat onto the premises — 「工房内では一切小麦を使わない」 and 「小麦は一切持ち込まないアレルギー対応の専用工房」 — with items labeled アレルギー28品目対応 and many marketed as 卵・乳・小麦不使用, explicitly aimed at children with wheat allergy. Because no wheat exists in the facility, gluten cross-contact risk is very low; the main caveat is that some baked goods do contain egg or dairy (so read each label), and shared display/handling is within an all-GF environment.
places.vegan_detail: Several items are fully plant-based, notably the 丸パン rolls (卵・乳・小麦不使用) and the baked ソルガムドーナツ (小麦・乳・卵不使用, made with organic soy milk), and these are labeled ヴィーガン. However many other products contain egg or dairy — the scones use 平飼い卵 (free-range eggs), and the chiffon, canelé, egg-mayo sandwich and あんバター are not vegan. Check each item's label.
places.hours_raw: Wed & Fri 10:00–16:00; Sat, Sun & holidays 10:00–15:00; closed Mon, Tue, Thu
places.neighborhood: 若里 (Wakasato), Nagano City — ~15 min walk from Nagano Station east exit
places.cuisine: Gluten-free bakery (sorghum & rice flour bread, scones, baked donuts)
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] A rare true celiac-safe destination: a wheat-free specialty workshop that never brings wheat on-site, uses sorghum + rice flour, and labels items to Japan's 28-allergen standard. Ideal safe stop for a celiac child near Nagano Station. Bakery/takeout focus with limited weekday hours (closed Mon/Tue/Thu) — plan around Wed/Fri/Sat/Sun; whole/Christmas cakes are order-ahead.

chef_bio.background: 縁-enishi- is run by shop manager Misari Yamaguchi, who herself suffered ill health from gluten — at age 25 she fell ill and learned that wheat did not suit her body (「25歳のときに体調を崩し、小麦が合わない体質だと判明」). She then self-taught gluten-free and rice-flour baking and trained (修業) at a gluten-free café in Osaka, and that lived experience is reflected in the products and the shop's design. The parent company, 信大発ベンチャー AKEBONO Co., Ltd., is led by CEO Itaru Inoue (井上 格), a former Tokyo trading-company employee who discovered sorghum while searching for alternative foods for his eldest son's wheat allergy; he founded the Shinshu Sorghum Promotion Association (2018) and AKEBONO (2019, certified a Shinshu University venture in 2020). The shop opened in Nagano's Wakasato district on 8 April 2023.
chef_bio.philosophy: The name 縁 (enishi, 'ties/connection') reflects Yamaguchi's belief in the importance of human connection — feeling in Osaka that 'I exist because of others,' she wished the shop to be a place that cherishes bonds and where new opportunities are born from those ties. The broader mission is body-kind, allergy-safe food so wheat-allergic children and gluten-sensitive people can eat with joy.
chef_bio.specialty: Sorghum (Shinshu-grown) and rice-flour gluten-free bread, scones and baked donuts

anecdotes.text: 「25歳のときに体調を崩し、小麦が合わない体質だと判明。その後、グルテンフリーと米粉を独学し、大阪のグルテンフリーカフェで修業しました」

CEO Itaru Inoue moved from a Tokyo trading company to Nagano and discovered sorghum while seeking wheat-free alternatives for his son's wheat allergy, founding the Shinshu Sorghum Promotion Association and AKEBONO.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: ナガラボ（長野シティプロモーション）の店長インタビュー、Web-Komachi のオープン紹介記事、PR TIMES のプレスリリースから、店長・山口美佐里氏の経歴（グルテン不耐・大阪での修業）と、代表・井上格氏／AKEBONO株式会社（信大発ベンチャー）による信州産ソルガム事業の背景を確認。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 「工房内では一切小麦を使わない、三大アレルゲンフリーの丸パン」 — the workshop uses no wheat at all; rolls are free of the three major allergens.
「小麦は一切持ち込まないアレルギー対応の専用工房」— a dedicated allergy-response workshop into which wheat is never brought; products labeled アレルギー28品目対応.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Bakery/sweets shop with no wheat on premises; wheat-based soy sauce is not a relevant ingredient here, and the workshop is wheat-free by policy.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Egg and dairy ARE used in some products (scones use 平飼い卵; chiffon/canelé/あんバター contain egg or butter), so vegan and egg/dairy-allergic visitors must read labels even though the shop is fully wheat-free.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Shop manager Misari Yamaguchi has a personal wheat-intolerance background and trained at a gluten-free café; founder Itaru Inoue started the sorghum business for his wheat-allergic son — allergy safety is core to the shop's purpose.

positives.text: Dedicated gluten-free specialty shop marketed as safe for wheat-allergic children, with items labeled to Japan's 28-allergen standard (アレルギー28品目対応) and 卵・乳・小麦不使用 options.
Confirmed open (opened 8 April 2023); Tabelog rating 3.00, address 長野県長野市若里2-1-23, phone 026-466-6610.

places.name: Cafe Fuwafuwa (Cafe ふわふわ)
places.gf_detail: A rice-flour pancake specialty café — every pancake is 米粉100%, so there is no wheat flour and no wheat pasta on the premises (the seed's 'shared pasta menu' does not exist; the savory items are also rice-flour pancakes). The venue's own claim: 「100％米粉を使った、ふわふわのパンケーキ」 and 「パンケーキは、米粉100%使用と砂糖を減らすことにより…罪悪感なく食べられるギルトフリー」. IMPORTANT caveat for celiac: this is a 'gluten-free pancake' shop, NOT a certified celiac kitchen, and no formal cross-contact protocol is published — the sweet pancakes (berry, fruit, caramel-nut, kids) are the reliably GF core, but savory pancakes such as the ハーブ鶏の照り焼き (herb-chicken teriyaki) and 信州サーモン use teriyaki/soy-based sauces that are wheat unless tamari, so confirm the sauce or stick to the sweet fruit pancakes.
places.vegan_detail: Dairy is avoided — the whipped cream is soy-milk based (豆乳ホイップ) — and organic fruit/veg are used. However the pancake batter contains egg (free-range 平飼い卵), so the pancakes themselves are not vegan. Only drinks such as the craft cola are reliably plant-based; ask before ordering if strict vegan.
places.hours_raw: Wed 12:00–16:00 / Thu–Sun 11:30–17:00 (last order 16:00). Closed Mon & Tue plus irregular days off (check Instagram for opening days).
places.neighborhood: 大町市大町・文化会館サン・アルプス大町棟内 (Omachi, near 北大町駅 / Omachi Cultural Hall, on the Hakuba route)
places.notes: [GF] Rice-flour pancake café in the Sun Alps Omachi cultural hall — a convenient GF sweet stop on the Omachi→Hakuba drive. The entire pancake range is 米粉100% (100% rice flour) with soy-milk whipped cream, so a celiac 13-year-old can safely order the sweet fruit/berry/caramel-nut/kids pancakes. Avoid or verify the sauce on savory pancakes (teriyaki/soy = wheat). No published celiac cross-contact protocol, so mention celiac disease and ask them to keep the plate free of soy-sauce toppings.

chef_bio.background: Owner Atsushi Komiyama opened Cafe Fuwafuwa on 30 June 2022 inside the Sun Alps Omachi building of the Omachi Cultural Hall. The concept is a 'guilt-free' (ギルトフリー) pancake shop built entirely on 100% rice flour rather than wheat, using carefully selected low-pesticide rice flour and Nagano-grown red sorghum (赤たかきび) flour, soy-milk whipped cream in place of dairy, and free-range non-GMO-fed eggs from the Aida cooperative. The reduced-sugar, rice-flour recipe was designed so the pancakes have roughly half the usual calories and can be eaten without guilt, with a stated aim of being body-friendly and family-friendly (the space has a nursing room and raised kids seating).
chef_bio.philosophy: Body-friendly, guilt-free pancakes: 100% rice flour + reduced sugar + organic/local ingredients so families and gluten-avoidant guests can eat comfortably.
chef_bio.specialty: 米粉100% (100% rice flour) fluffy pancakes, sweet and savory, with soy-milk whipped cream

anecdotes.text: 「パンケーキは、米粉100%使用と砂糖を減らすことにより、当社比にてパンケーキのカロリーは半分程となり、罪悪感なく食べられるギルトフリーとなっております。」
Uses carefully selected rice flour plus Nagano-grown red sorghum (赤たかきび) flour, soy-milk whipped cream instead of dairy milk, and free-range non-GMO-fed eggs.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Japanese sources (official site cafe-fuwafuwa.com, Web-Komachi, echohall.jp, 北アルプスCOOL便 nagano-ken.jp blog, Tabelog) consistently describe a June-2022 rice-flour pancake café by owner 小宮山敦 emphasizing 米粉100%, guilt-free/reduced-sugar recipes, soy-milk cream and local Nagano ingredients; none publish a formal celiac cross-contact protocol.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Entire pancake range is 米粉100% (100% rice flour); no wheat flour or wheat pasta is used on the premises, so batter-level cross-contact is inherently low. However no formal celiac cross-contact protocol is published.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Savory pancakes such as ハーブ鶏の照り焼き (herb-chicken teriyaki, described as 甘じょっぱい / sweet-salty) and the Shinshu salmon plate use teriyaki/soy-based sauces that are wheat unless tamari — a celiac should confirm the sauce or choose the sweet pancakes.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Whipped cream is soy-milk based (dairy-free) but pancake batter contains free-range egg, so pancakes are not vegan.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No published allergy/celiac handling protocol found; the shop markets 'gluten-free pancakes' but is not a certified celiac kitchen. Communicate celiac disease directly at ordering.

positives.text: Concept is 100% rice flour with no wheat flour on premises, plus reduced sugar, soy-milk cream, organic local produce and Nagano red sorghum — a genuinely rice-flour-first kitchen, unusual on the Hakuba route.

places.name: Harutonari (ハルトナリ。)
places.gf_detail: A gluten-free sweets café (opened 13 April 2025) whose baked goods are all made with Nagano-grown rice flour and no wheat. Local press describes it plainly as 「グルテンフリーのカフェ」 and 「小麦粉を使わない米粉のスイーツ」 (a gluten-free café; rice-flour sweets that use no wheat flour), and the cakes are also white-sugar-free. Because the entire food menu is rice-flour based this reads as an effectively wheat-free kitchen; the main residual gluten source on premises is bottled beer offered as a drink, so a celiac should still confirm no shared utensils touch the beer or any brought-in items.
places.vegan_detail: Cakes, tarts, roll cakes, prune caramel and quiche use eggs and dairy, so most sweets are not vegan. Drinks such as the house craft cola (¥580) and black coffee are plant-based, so a vegan can drink but rarely eat here. Ask staff which of the rotating 3-4 sweets, if any, are dairy/egg free.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–17:30 (Sun until 16:30), last order at close; closed Thursdays plus one more day per week
places.neighborhood: 中央3丁目・なかまちのなか中村屋 (Nakamachi/Chuo 3, Matsumoto)
places.notes: [GF] Effectively wheat-free café: every sweet is rice flour, no wheat flour, and white-sugar-free — a comfortable, low-risk stop for a celiac kid wanting cake and a drink. Space is tiny (about 10 seats) and staff ask for groups of 1-2, so expect a short wait. Beer/sparkling wine are the only gluten on site; keep them away from a celiac's plate.

chef_bio.background: Owner Izumi Matsuoka left a long office career to open Harutonari, driven by a personal love of café-hopping and a wish to 「来てくれた人が幸せになる場所を作りたい」 (create a place where visitors become happy). She trained through pop-up (kashi-kari) stints — about three months from January 2023 at the café Antares, then roughly a year of weekly service at the Portuguese restaurant Fernando — before opening her own storefront on 13 April 2025. The shop sits inside the renovated 「なかまちのなか中村屋」 complex near the Metoba River in the Nakamachi district. The name comes from 「春隣」, a winter season-word evoking the approach of spring after hard cold, chosen for the relaxed warmth she wants guests to feel.
chef_bio.philosophy: Gluten-free, white-sugar-free rice-flour confections in a calm antique-furnished space; a warm neighbourhood refuge.
chef_bio.specialty: Nagano rice-flour cakes, roll cakes and tarts with seasonal fruit (menu rotates every 1-2 months)

anecdotes.text: The interior is furnished with old tools reflecting the owner's love of antiques — a table remade from an old treadle sewing-machine base and chairs once used in a European church.
She plans to add light meals using rice-flour bread and to explore evening service.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Matsumoto Keizai Shimbun (2025-06-06), MG Press and Web-Komachi opening features, and the Nakamachi shopping-district member page all describe a gluten-free rice-flour café opened April 2025 by Izumi Matsuoka in the Nakamachi-no-Naka Nakamuraya complex; corroborated by the gf-akane wheat-allergy blogger's Matsumoto GF tour.

gf_cross_contamination.text: All food is rice-flour based with no wheat flour used, so baking cross-contact risk is low; bottled beer/sparkling wine on premises is the main gluten source and should be kept away from a celiac's food.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Sweets café; no soy-sauce-based savoury dishes at time of research (planned rice-bread light meals not yet confirmed).

vegan_cross_contact.text: Cakes use eggs and dairy; no dedicated vegan prep noted.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Featured on a wheat-allergy blogger's Matsumoto gluten-free tour as a GF café; owner intends the shop as a safe rice-flour space.

positives.text: Entire sweets menu is gluten-free and white-sugar-free using Nagano rice flour.

places.name: Hibi Donut (日々どーなつ 松本中町店)
places.gf_detail: A donut specialty shop whose own signage reads 「米粉と小麦粉のドーナツ専門店」 (a rice-flour AND wheat-flour donut shop). Only the 米粉 old-fashioned line is wheat-free — the venue describes it as 「米粉を用いて外はサクサク、中はしっとりとした『オールドファッション』」 and a local GF blog confirms 「米粉のオールドファッションドーナツを購入できます。それ以外のドーナツは小麦を使用していますので、アレルギー表示を必ずご確認ください」. Because yeast-raised wheat donuts (とろ生クリーム, etc.) are fried and made in the same small kitchen, no dedicated fryer or dedicated GF line is confirmed — cross-contact is a real risk for a celiac. Options only; ask whether the 米粉 donuts are fried in separate oil before buying.
places.vegan_detail: Donuts are made with dairy and egg; the yeast line is cream-filled. No vegan items advertised. Not suitable for a strict vegan.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–17:00 (closes when sold out; ~500/day limit). Closed Mon, Tue, Thu. Closures also announced on Instagram.
places.neighborhood: 中町通り (Nakamachi-dori), 松本市中央 (Chuo, Matsumoto City)
places.notes: [GF] Only the 米粉 (rice-flour) old-fashioned donuts are wheat-free — plain ~¥230, double chocolate ~¥270, plus kinako and seasonal matcha flavors. The shop also fries wheat-flour yeast donuts in the same kitchen, so no dedicated fryer is confirmed; for a celiac child, confirm separate frying oil and ask staff to check the allergen label before purchase. Sells out early (500/day cap), so go before mid-afternoon.

chef_bio.background: 日々どーなつ is operated by ねんりん (Nenrin), a Nagano company that runs several sweets shops in the prefecture, under owner Yoshitsugu Miyashita. The Matsumoto Nakamachi shop opened March 11, 2025 in the former premises of おいも日和 (Oimo Biyori), a sweet-potato specialty shop that had run ~8 years on Nakamachi-dori before closing at the end of January 2025. It is the second 日々どーなつ location, following the original in Nagano City's Shinonoi district. The rice and wheat are largely grown on the company's own farm in Sakaki-machi and milled in-house, and donuts are hand-made and sold fresh daily.
chef_bio.philosophy: 『毎日食べられるドーナツ』 — donuts you can eat every day, made from self-cultivated, in-house-milled ingredients grown on the company farm.
chef_bio.specialty: 自社製粉の米粉オールドファッション (in-house-milled rice-flour old-fashioned donuts) alongside wheat-flour yeast donuts.

anecdotes.text: The shop bills itself as 「米粉と小麦粉のドーナツ専門店」 and caps production at roughly 500 donuts per day, made fresh in-store and sold until gone.
Occupies the former おいも日和 sweet-potato shop, which had operated about 8 years on Nakamachi-dori before closing end of January 2025.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 松本経済新聞 (local press) and Web-Komachi report ねんりん/宮下義継 opened 日々どーなつ 松本中町店 on 2025-03-11 in the former おいも日和 space, using self-farmed, in-house-milled 米粉 and 小麦粉; the 米粉 old-fashioned line is described as 小麦粉不使用 (wheat-flour-free).

gf_cross_contamination.text: Shop makes both 米粉 (wheat-free) and 小麦粉 (wheat) donuts in the same small kitchen; no dedicated GF fryer or line is stated by the venue, so cross-contact is plausible.
GF blogger warns: 「それ以外のドーナツは小麦を使用していますので、アレルギー表示を必ずご確認ください」 (other donuts use wheat — always check the allergen label) and does not confirm dedicated equipment.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No published allergen-handling protocol; blogger advises confirming allergen labels in person. Ask directly about separate frying oil for the 米粉 donuts.

positives.text: The 米粉 old-fashioned donuts are made with in-house-milled rice flour and are described as 小麦粉不使用 (wheat-flour-free).

places.name: Jinenya Karuizawa Bypass (じねんや 軽井沢バイパス店)
places.gf_detail: Organic macrobiotic vegan restaurant/shop where roughly one-third of the menu is gluten-free (玄米ご飯・米粉ベースの和洋メニュー). The venue markets itself as 「グルテンフリー・アレルギー対応」, but the JP celiac guide empacede warns: 「小麦粉を使ったメニューと、調理器具、食器を共用しているので、重篤な小麦アレルギーの場合は、お店の方に相談した方がよいです。」 The kitchen and dishware are SHARED with wheat dishes (they also serve wheat pasta), so this is options-level, not celiac-dedicated — cross-contact is a real risk and you must declare celiac and consult staff in advance. Confirm soy sauce/miso are wheat-free (tamari) for any specific dish.
places.vegan_detail: A macrobiotic / 和食ビーガン restaurant run by a couple; the menu is plant-based (organic vegetables, brown rice, yuba, soy). Vegewel and the shop's own site list it as vegan/vegetarian/macrobiotic. Nearly the entire menu is vegan-suitable; a few egg-containing items (e.g. スパニッシュオムレツ) are the exception, so confirm per dish.
places.hours_raw: 10:00–20:00 (summer 9:30–20:00), closed Thu. Reservation required after 18:00.
places.cuisine: Organic macrobiotic vegan / Japanese and Western
places.notes: [GF] — Macrobiotic vegan spot with a genuine gluten-free sub-menu (~1/3 of dishes, on the 米粉・玄米 side), but the kitchen and dishware are shared with wheat items so it is options-level for celiacs, not dedicated. For a 13-year-old celiac, phone ahead, say セリアック病, and have them plate a naturally GF set (玄米ちらし, せいろ蒸し野菜, 花かご弁当) with tamari; avoid the pasta/pancake side of the menu and ask them to use clean utensils.

chef_bio.background: じねんや is a Nagano-based organic/natural-food brand; the Karuizawa Bypass branch pairs a natural-foods shop (organic vegetables, seasonings, macrobiotic goods, natural cosmetics) with an adjoining restaurant serving organic, macrobiotic, plant-based dishes. Vegewel describes it as a resort-town shop run by a married couple offering visually beautiful 和食ビーガン cuisine built on organic vegetables and brown rice. The kitchen emphasizes macrobiotic principles and allergy accommodation on request.
chef_bio.philosophy: Macrobiotic, plant-based cooking centered on organic vegetables, brown rice and seasonal produce, with allergy/gluten-free accommodation for guests who ask in advance.
chef_bio.specialty: Macrobiotic vegan set meals — 花かご弁当, 玄米五目ちらし寿司, せいろ蒸し野菜, 豆乳リゾット.

anecdotes.text: Vegewel profiles it as a couple-run shop in the Karuizawa resort area serving 目にも美しい和食ビーガン.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Own wixsite site markets オーガニック・マクロビ・ヴィーガン・グルテンフリー・アレルギー対応; empacede rates it ★★★ and flags the shared kitchen; Tabelog/Vegewel confirm organic macrobiotic vegan, couple-run.

gf_cross_contamination.text: 「小麦粉を使ったメニューと、調理器具、食器を共用しているので、重篤な小麦アレルギーの場合は、お店の方に相談した方がよいです。」— shared cooking equipment and dishware with wheat dishes.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Not explicitly documented whether tamari is used; standard shoyu contains wheat — confirm per dish.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Menu is plant-based/macrobiotic; a few egg items exist (e.g. スパニッシュオムレツ), so vegan guests should confirm per dish.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Site states allergy accommodation is available if requested in advance (アレルギー対応・事前申告).

positives.text: Roughly one-third of the menu is gluten-free, built on brown rice / rice flour, and empacede rates it ★★★.

places.name: Menshou Mabushiya Matsumoto (炙り味噌らーめん 麺匠真武咲弥 松本店)
places.gf_detail: This roasted-miso ramen shop (a small Sapporo/Shibuya/Azumino chain) offers a brown-rice-noodle ramen advertised as 「玄米麺を使用したグルテンフリーのラーメン」 and a GF karaage. The critical caveat: the wheat-allergy blogger reports the GF noodles are 「基本的に小麦の麺と一緒に茹でる」 (by default boiled in the same water as wheat noodles) unless you ask them to 「窯を分けてくれる」 (use a separate pot). Miso base can contain barley and standard soy sauce contains wheat, so a celiac must explicitly request a separate boil, tamari/GF seasoning confirmation, and a clean fryer for the karaage — otherwise cross-contact is high in this shared wheat-ramen kitchen.
places.vegan_detail: The chain lists an オリエンタルベジタリアン (oriental vegetarian) option alongside the GF item, but ramen broths are typically animal-based and 'oriental vegetarian' still excludes only alliums, not necessarily all animal products. A strict vegan must confirm broth and toppings; do not assume the GF brown-rice ramen is vegan.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–14:30, 17:00–19:45; closed 3rd Wednesday
places.neighborhood: 高宮北（西松本駅近く）(Takamiya-kita, near Nishi-Matsumoto Stn)
places.notes: [GF] Ordinary wheat-ramen shop that happens to offer a brown-rice GF ramen and GF karaage — genuinely 'options', not a safe kitchen by default. Must ask for a separate boiling pot (窯を分ける), tamari/GF seasoning, and a clean fryer. For a celiac 13-year-old this is only worthwhile if staff clearly understand the separate-pot request; when in doubt, skip.

chef_bio.origin: Sapporo-style roasted-miso ramen brand (Samurai Noodle / Shinbusakiya)
chef_bio.background: 真武咲弥 (Shinbusakiya) is a roasted-miso (炙り味噌) ramen brand with branches in Sapporo, Shibuya, Azumino and this Matsumoto shop on Route 19 in the Takamiya-kita area near Nishi-Matsumoto Station. The kitchen serves standard wheat ramen but has added a brown-rice-noodle GF line and an 'oriental vegetarian' option to broaden accessibility. No individual owner/chef biography is documented in Japanese sources for the Matsumoto branch, so bio detail is deliberately light.
chef_bio.philosophy: Roasted-miso ramen with inclusive menu lines (GF brown-rice noodle, oriental vegetarian).
chef_bio.specialty: Aburi (roasted) miso ramen; oversized karaage

anecdotes.text: The GF option uses a firm, square-cut medium-thick brown-rice noodle (玄米麺).

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: The gf-akane wheat-allergy blogger reviews the Matsumoto branch's GF brown-rice ramen and notes the shared-pot boiling default and separate-pot accommodation; Tabelog and local guides confirm address (高宮北3-1), hours and 3rd-Wed closure. Single-blogger sourcing on the GF specifics → light bio, low confidence.

gf_cross_contamination.text: GF brown-rice noodles are by default boiled in the same water as wheat noodles unless a separate pot is requested — high cross-contact risk without asking.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Soy-sauce (shoyu) ramen and standard seasonings likely contain wheat; miso base may contain barley — confirm tamari/GF seasoning before ordering.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Oriental vegetarian line exists but broth is not confirmed vegan; shared kitchen with meat and wheat.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Staff will use a separate boiling pot on request and were flexible (e.g. separating the spicy miso condiment) per the blogger.

positives.text: A dedicated GF brown-rice-noodle ramen and GF karaage are on the regular menu, and staff accommodate a separate-pot request.

places.name: Wafu no Yado Masuya (和風の宿 ますや)
places.gf_detail: A 140-year-old hot-spring ryokan in Yudanaka Onsen (the practical base for the snow-monkey park) serving 郷土会席 kaiseki dinner and breakfast. It publishes NO standing gluten-free claim — the official site only states 「旬の食材を吟味し料理長が真心を込めた…四季折々の山の幸・川の幸にこだわった郷土会席料理」 — but per the seed it will accommodate celiacs on advance request: the kitchen can use a gluten-free miso specifically for celiac guests and offer gluten-free tempura alternatives across the multi-course meal. This is arrange-in-advance, negotiated GF (ask), not a documented program; standard kaiseki soy sauce/miso/tempura contain wheat, so every course must be pre-confirmed with the head chef and cross-contact in a shared kitchen assumed.
places.vegan_detail: Traditional kaiseki built on seasonal mountain and river ingredients (fish, likely dashi). No published vegan menu; plant-based meals would have to be arranged directly with the ryokan well in advance.
places.hours_raw: Lodging (ryokan). Dinner and breakfast included. Check-in 15:00 / check-out 10:00 (approx.).
places.notes: [GF] — Not a GF-dedicated venue, but the near-only celiac-workable dinner+breakfast option at the snow-monkey base: a source-flowing-onsen ryokan whose kitchen will, on advance arrangement, prepare a celiac guest a careful multi-course meal using gluten-free miso and gluten-free tempura substitutes. For a 13-year-old celiac, book direct, state セリアック病 clearly at reservation, and confirm each course (tamari not wheat shoyu, GF miso, no wheat-flour tempura/thickeners) with the 料理長 before arrival. Treat as ask until confirmed in writing.

chef_bio.origin: 山ノ内町 湯田中温泉, Nagano
chef_bio.background: 和風の宿 ますや is a traditional Japanese ryokan founded over 140 years ago in the historic Yudanaka Onsen district of Yamanouchi, drawing 源泉掛け流し water from four hot-spring sources with two open-air baths. Meals are 郷土会席 kaiseki prepared by the ryokan's head chef (料理長) from seasonal mountain and river ingredients. It sits within walking distance of Shibu Onsen and about a 20-minute walk / 3-minute taxi from Yudanaka Station, close to the Jigokudani snow-monkey park.
chef_bio.philosophy: Seasonal regional kaiseki — 「旬の食材を吟味し料理長が真心を込めた」 — with hospitality flexible enough to accommodate dietary needs arranged in advance.
chef_bio.specialty: 郷土会席 (regional kaiseki) using local 山の幸・川の幸; onsen hospitality for snow-monkey visitors.

anecdotes.text: Official site: 「旬の食材を吟味し料理長が真心を込めた物をご提供しております。四季折々の山の幸・川の幸にこだわった郷土会席料理」.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Official site, Jalan, Ikyu and Tabelog confirm a 140-year-old source-flowing Yudanaka onsen ryokan serving chef-prepared regional kaiseki; none publish a gluten-free program, so GF is by advance private arrangement only.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Shared ryokan kitchen with standard wheat-containing kaiseki components; no documented GF protocol — assume cross-contact and pre-confirm every course.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard kaiseki uses wheat shoyu and barley/wheat miso; per seed the kitchen can swap in a gluten-free miso for celiac guests on request — confirm tamari for shoyu too.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Kaiseki is fish/dashi-based; no vegan menu published — arrange directly if needed.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Per seed, celiac-attentive on advance request — GF miso and GF tempura alternatives across a careful multi-course meal; not a published program, so book direct and confirm.

positives.text: Best (near-only) celiac dinner+breakfast option at the snow-monkey base; willing to accommodate gluten-free with advance arrangement.

places.name: nico-simply (ニコ シンプリー)
places.gf_detail: Organic Italian restaurant whose two mains — 麻炭天然酵母ピザ (hemp-charcoal natural-yeast pizza) and 生パスタ (fresh durum-wheat pasta) — are both wheat-based by default, but can be swapped to gluten-free versions for an extra charge. The curated JP celiac guide empacede rates it ★★★★★ and states: 「ピザとパスタをメインとするレストランで、追加料金でピザとパスタをグルテンフリーにすることができます。」(a restaurant centred on pizza and pasta where, for an additional charge, the pizza and pasta can be made gluten-free). No source documents a separate GF prep line, dedicated oven or dedicated boiling water, and wheat pizza/pasta are made in the same kitchen — so this is an on-request substitution, not a celiac-safe kitchen. Confirm cross-contact and preparation directly before ordering for a celiac diner.
places.vegan_detail: Vegan (ヴィーガン) diners are explicitly accommodated. The lunchtime salad bar (サラダバーランチ) is built to suit both gluten-free and vegan diets using carefully chosen seasonings and herbs, and the kitchen advertises 無添加/オーガニック cooking with 「化学調味料、白砂糖は一切使用していません」(no chemical seasonings and no white sugar). Vegan pizza/pasta variants are available; confirm cheese-free and dairy-free options when ordering.
places.hours_raw: Lunch 11:30–14:30 (Mon, Wed–Sun); dinner 18:00–21:00 (Fri, Sat, Sun). Closed Tuesdays.
places.neighborhood: 吉田 (Yoshida), 中野市 (Nakano City), Nagano — on the Nagano → Snow Monkey (地獄谷) route
places.cuisine: Organic Italian — natural-yeast pizza, fresh pasta, salad bar
places.notes: [GF] — GF pizza and pasta are available only as a paid substitution in a shared kitchen that also makes wheat pizza (natural-yeast) and durum-wheat fresh pasta; treat as options, not celiac-safe. Best celiac bet is the salad bar lunch, which is designed to suit GF and vegan diets. Call ahead (0269-23-5333) to confirm GF prep and cross-contact for a coeliac child before relying on the pizza/pasta swap.

chef_bio.background: nico-simply is an owner-run Natural Food & Drink restaurant in Yoshida, Nakano City, positioned along the Nagano-to-Snow-Monkey (Jigokudani) tourist route. It centres on 麻炭天然酵母ピザ (hemp-charcoal natural-yeast pizza) and chewy 生パスタ (fresh pasta), plus a salad bar heavy on local Shinshu vegetables and mushrooms. The kitchen's stated ethos is additive-free, organic cooking with 「化学調味料、白砂糖は一切使用していません」(absolutely no chemical seasonings or white sugar). The shop also sells hemp/CBD foods and goods and offers dog-friendly terrace seating, reflecting a broader natural-living concept.
chef_bio.philosophy: Additive-free, organic, locally sourced cooking (無添加・オーガニック) that anyone — including gluten-free and vegan diners — can enjoy, using carefully chosen seasonings and herbs rather than chemical seasonings or white sugar.
chef_bio.specialty: 麻炭天然酵母ピザ (hemp-charcoal natural-yeast pizza) and 生パスタ (fresh pasta), with a GF/vegan-friendly salad bar lunch.

anecdotes.text: Roughly 200 kinds of drinks are on offer alongside the food, and live band performances are sometimes held in the space.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 食べログ・ぐるなび・信州なかの観光・ATRASHOME の各記事は、地元野菜ときのこをたっぷり使った無添加・オーガニックのイタリアン店であること、麻炭天然酵母ピザと生パスタが看板であること、化学調味料・白砂糖不使用であることを伝えるが、オーナーシェフの氏名や創業年は明記していない。

gf_cross_contamination.text: GF pizza and pasta are provided only as a paid substitution; the same kitchen makes wheat natural-yeast pizza and durum-wheat fresh pasta, and no dedicated GF prep line, oven or boiling water is documented — cross-contact risk is real.
「ピザとパスタをメインとするレストランで、追加料金でピザとパスタをグルテンフリーにすることができます。」— GF is described as an add-on swap, not a separate celiac-safe kitchen.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No tamari/wheat-free soy statement published; Italian menu, but confirm sauces on the salad bar and any dressings for wheat-based soy or seasonings.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Salad bar lunch is explicitly built to suit both gluten-free and vegan diets with carefully selected seasonings and herbs.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Staff will make pizza/pasta gluten-free on request for an extra charge, indicating willingness to accommodate; degree of cross-contact care is not documented — verify by phone.

positives.text: ★★★★★ top rating in the empacede JP gluten-free guide for making both pizza and pasta GF on request.
Additive-free, organic kitchen using no chemical seasonings and no white sugar, with GF/vegan-friendly salad bar.

places.name: Ramen Sen (ラーメン仙)
places.gf_detail: A no-MSG, additive-free (無添加・地産地消) ramen shop that actively advertises allergy accommodation: the standard noodle is wheat-and-tapioca, but for +¥220 you can swap to a gluten-free noodle, and the shop states it can make a wheat-free bowl — Vegewel lists it as 「グルテンフリーの麺も用意しております」 and one press piece confirms 「卵不使用、小麦不使用のアレルギー対策もできます」 (egg-free and wheat-free accommodation is possible). Critically for celiac, this is a wheat-ramen kitchen: the broths use locally-sourced soy sauce and miso (地産地消) with no published tamari/wheat-free claim, and no source confirms a dedicated pot or separate boiling water for the GF noodle. Treat the GF noodle swap and the wheat-free bowl as an on-request accommodation — you must specifically ask for a 小麦不使用 (wheat-free) tare/broth AND confirm a dedicated pot before ordering.
places.vegan_detail: The shop describes 「壁のないラーメンづくりをしており、肉、魚系とヴィーガンのラーメンをつくってます」 (barrier-free ramen-making — they make meat/fish and vegan ramen), and Vegewel tags it ビーガンメニューあり / ベジタリアンメニューあり. A vegan ramen is a standing menu option, but confirm the vegan bowl's tare is also fish/meat-free on the day.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–15:00, 17:00–21:00 (Tue–Sun); closed Mondays plus irregular closures; closes when sold out. Some listings show lunch last order 14:30 / dinner 20:00–21:30 — hours vary.
places.neighborhood: 穂高 (Hotaka), 安曇野市 / Azumino City, Nagano
places.notes: [GF] Wheat-ramen kitchen that will make an allergy bowl: swap to a gluten-free noodle for +¥220 and request a 小麦不使用 (wheat-free) broth. Because the regular noodle is wheat and the standard broths use local (likely wheat) soy sauce, a celiac MUST (1) order the GF noodle, (2) explicitly ask for the wheat-free tare/soup, and (3) confirm the GF noodle is boiled in a separate/dedicated pot — none of the sources confirm a dedicated pot. Cash and QR-code pay accepted; no reservations; ~800 m / 4 min drive from Hotaka Station.

chef_bio.background: Ramen Sen is a chef-owned shop in Hotaka, Azumino, built on a concept of 無添加と地産地消 (no additives and local sourcing). Japanese listings note the owner honed his craft at the regional ramen shop きまぐれ八兵衛 (Kimagure Hachibei) before opening his own 「唯一無二のラーメン」 (one-and-only ramen). The kitchen deliberately avoids chemical seasonings and builds broths from Nagano ingredients — Shinshu salmon, Shinshu pork, and locally-made soy sauce and miso.
chef_bio.philosophy: Additive-free, MSG-free, local-ingredient ramen made 'without barriers' (壁のないラーメンづくり) so that vegan, vegetarian, egg-allergy and wheat-allergy guests can all eat.
chef_bio.specialty: Shinshu salmon ramen (信州サーモンらーめん) — broth made from 100% Nagano-farmed Shinshu salmon; also Shinshu-pork tonkotsu and a vegan ramen.

anecdotes.text: Uses 100% Nagano-farmed Shinshu salmon for the signature salmon-broth ramen and 100% Shinshu pork for the tonkotsu.
Popular with women and families; offers a children's ramen (~¥200) and side dishes.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Deli-koma と oishii-shinshu が『無添加と地産地消』『無化調の優しい味づくり』とコンセプトを紹介し、店主はきまぐれ八兵衛出身と記載。Vegewel と Yahoo!ニュース（くりとも）が『卵不使用・小麦不使用のアレルギー対応』『ヴィーガンのラーメン』『グルテンフリーの麺も用意』と明記。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat-ramen kitchen — the standard noodle is wheat-and-tapioca and no source confirms a dedicated pot or separate boiling water for the gluten-free noodle. Assume shared-water cross-contact unless staff confirm otherwise.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Broths are built on locally-sourced soy sauce and miso (地産地消) with no published tamari or wheat-free-soy claim; standard broths likely contain wheat via soy sauce. The advertised 小麦不使用 (wheat-free) bowl must be requested explicitly.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Vegan and vegetarian ramen are made in the same kitchen as fish/meat broths; confirm the vegan tare/soup on the day.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Shop explicitly advertises allergy accommodation: 「卵不使用、小麦不使用のアレルギー対策もできます」 (egg-free and wheat-free accommodation possible) and 「グルテンフリーの麺も用意しております」.

positives.text: MSG-free / additive-free (無化調・無添加) kitchen with a stated 'barrier-free' ethos that welcomes wheat-allergy, egg-allergy and vegan guests — an unusually allergy-aware ramen shop.
Gluten-free noodle available as a standing swap for +¥220.

places.name: Riz Karuizawa (リズ カルイザワ)
places.gf_detail: A dedicated 米粉（rice-flour）gluten-free bakery in Karuizawa's Lake New Town — everything sold is gluten-free, with NO wheat flour used on the premises. The shop bakes 「グルテンフリーのパン」from 米粉 (specially-cultivated Koshihikari rice flour) with 白神こだま酵母; one source notes some breads also blend 大豆粉 (soy flour), which is still gluten-free. The owner trained under 大塚せつ子, a pioneer of Japanese rice-flour baking (「米粉の母」). Because it is a rice-flour-only kitchen, cross-contact risk is very low, but an allergen card is posted for milk/soy, so celiac guests can also confirm dairy per item.
places.vegan_detail: A bakery, not fully vegan — the range includes meat items (bacon ears ¥330, wiener roll ¥390) and egg-based bakes (chiffon), so it is not a vegan venue. Plain rice-flour loaves and baguettes (baked with coconut oil / brown sugar) may be plant-based, but confirm egg/dairy per item.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–15:00; open Fri, Sat, Sun plus alternate Mondays. Closed Tue–Thu. Winter closure from December to mid-March (seasonal).
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] — A rice-flour-only bakery: every product is gluten-free and no wheat is baked on-site, so it is one of the safest celiac stops in Karuizawa for a 13-year-old. IMPORTANT: it is seasonal and short-hours — closed roughly December to mid-March, and open only Fri–Sun (plus alternate Mondays) 11:00–15:00, selling out early; check the @riz_karuizawa Instagram before going. Card payment is not accepted (cash / PayPay). Still confirm milk/egg per item for the sweeter bakes.

chef_bio.origin: 軽井沢町 発地, Nagano
chef_bio.background: Riz Karuizawa is a small, one-woman 米粉パン工房 in Karuizawa's Lake New Town (発地), opened in 2023. Owner Yamada Ritsuko trained under Otsuka Setsuko (大塚せつ子), regarded as a first-mover and 「米粉の母」 in Japan's rice-flour baking world. She bakes gluten-free breads and cakes from specially-cultivated Koshihikari rice flour, Shirakami-kodama yeast, brown sugar and coconut oil, aiming for moist, filling, lower-calorie rice bread.
chef_bio.philosophy: Commit fully to gluten-free — a rice-flour-only kitchen so celiac and wheat-allergic customers can eat everything on the shelf with confidence.
chef_bio.specialty: Rice-flour loaves, mini soft baguettes, cinnamon rolls, chiffon cups and cookies — all gluten-free.

anecdotes.text: The shop is profiled as run by a maker who studied under 大塚せつ子, the 「米粉の母」, and praised for moist, filling, low-calorie rice bread.
A local blogger titled her review 「グルテンフリーに徹するなら…」 — 'if you're going to commit fully to gluten-free' — underlining that it is a dedicated GF shop.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Web-Komachi, naganogourmetbekkan, simple-yuumin and pikyoblog all describe Riz as a dedicated 米粉100%/グルテンフリー bakery run by Yamada Ritsuko (trained under 大塚せつ子); short weekend-only, winter-closed hours; card payment unavailable.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Dedicated rice-flour bakery — no wheat flour used on premises, so cross-contact risk is very low.
Breads use 米粉 (Koshihikari rice flour) with 白神こだま酵母; some also blend 大豆粉 (soy flour), which is gluten-free.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Range includes meat (bacon ears, wiener roll) and egg bakes (chiffon); an allergen card for milk/soy is posted — confirm per item.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Allergen reference board (food/milk) available in-store.

positives.text: 100% rice-flour, gluten-free bakery; listed as a gluten-free bakery on FindMeGlutenFree for Karuizawa.

places.name: Roots Cafe Hakuba (ルーツカフェ)
places.gf_detail: English reviews (HappyCow, hakuba.com) report GF items — the vegetable curry, tortilla chips and pancakes are called gluten-free, baked goods use rice flour instead of wheat, and a GF pizza is mentioned. IMPORTANT: this GF claim is English-sourced ONLY; no Japanese-language source (Tabelog, Gnavi, HotPepper) establishes or even mentions gluten-free, so it cannot be confirmed and must be verified in person on arrival. It is a small, fully vegan shared kitchen where pizza/baked goods share a single oven and prep space, so cross-contact is a real risk. One reviewer noted staff were unsure which items contained gluten — treat GF as unverified until you confirm with the kitchen.
places.vegan_detail: 100% vegan cafe — reviewers repeatedly confirm 'everything is vegan' and it is described as Hakuba's only exclusively vegan restaurant. No meat, fish, dairy or egg is used; the kitchen builds dishes on local vegetables, soy protein, tofu and organic/fair-trade ingredients. Fully vegan does NOT mean gluten-free: soy 'karaage' is marinated with soy sauce (contains wheat unless tamari), and many items are wheat-based.
places.hours_raw: Summer (mid-July–late Sept): 10:00–16:30. Winter (mid-Dec–late March): 8:30–17:30. Evening hours during events. Closed in shoulder seasons; no fixed closed day (irregular).
places.neighborhood: 白馬村北城 (Hakuba, Happo-one international slopes, Kitaazumi District, Nagano)
places.cuisine: Vegan cafe / plant-based (burgers, curry, pizza, karaage)
places.notes: [GF] GF options exist per English sources only (curry, tortilla chips, pancakes, rice-flour bakes, a GF pizza) but this is NOT established in any Japanese source — verify every item with the kitchen on arrival. Small fully-vegan shared kitchen with a shared oven means cross-contact risk for a celiac; one review noted staff were unsure which items contained gluten. Soy karaage uses soy sauce (wheat) — ask for tamari-free confirmation. Seasonal venue: mainly winter, limited summer hours (mid-July–late Sept), closed shoulder seasons.

chef_bio.background: Owner of the cafe, which is operated in association with Evergreen Ski School on the Happo-One international slopes. He brought in a professional vegan chef and a Masters in Nutritional Health to improve the menu quality.
chef_bio.philosophy: Serve delicious food with a low carbon footprint — 'healing the earth one bite at a time' — using organic, locally-grown and fair-trade ingredients that are kind to the earth and the body.
chef_bio.specialty: Plant-based comfort food: Buddha burger, vegetable curry, soy karaage, vegan pizza.

anecdotes.text: Owner Dave Enright emphasized hiring a professional vegan chef and a Masters in Nutritional Health to raise the quality of the menu.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Japanese listings (Tabelog, Gnavi, HotPepper) confirm the cafe as a vegetarian/vegan cafe using local vegetables and organic/fair-trade food near Happo-one, but give no owner or chef biographical detail.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Small seasonal shared kitchen; pizza and baked goods share a single oven and prep space, creating cross-contact risk. A reviewer noted staff were uncertain which items contained gluten.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Soy 'karaage' is marinated overnight in garlic, salt koji, soy sauce and sake lees — standard soy sauce contains wheat unless tamari is used; verify.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fully vegan kitchen — no meat, fish, dairy or egg used, so animal cross-contact is not a concern.

staff_allergy_handling.text: GF knowledge appears inconsistent among staff; one visitor reported uncertainty about which items contained gluten.

positives.text: Explicitly lists gluten-free items (curry, tortilla chips, pancakes) and uses rice flour instead of wheat for baked goods.
100% vegan, organic and locally-grown ingredients.

places.name: Shinshu Juwari Soba Ten Karuizawa (信州十割そば てん 軽井沢店)
places.gf_detail: A 十割そば (100% buckwheat) specialist that opened July 2025 by JR Karuizawa station, and one of the best celiac soba finds in Nagano because — unlike the main-branch soba — BOTH the dipping sauce and the tempura are gluten-free. The shop states 「つなぎを一切使わない十割そば」 (no wheat binder), uses 木島平産そば粉100% with 打ち粉不使用 (no wheat dusting flour), serves 「本店と同じグルテンフリーのつゆ」, and makes tempura from 「米粉と蕎麦粉を合わせて揚げたグルテンフリーの天ぷら」 (rice + buckwheat flour, no wheat). Rated high rather than dedicated: it is a soba-only kitchen (no wheat udon/ramen), but public sources do not explicitly confirm a separate dedicated boiling pot, so a celiac should confirm 別鍋/専用茹で湯 on arrival.
places.vegan_detail: Buckwheat noodles and vegetable/mushroom tempura are plant-based, but the tsuyu is a standard dashi-based sauce (likely bonito), so most soba dishes are not vegan. Vegetable options (野菜そば, 舞茸天, 季節の野菜天) can suit vegans if you confirm the dashi and batter contain no fish/egg.
places.neighborhood: Karuizawa-higashi (Karuizawa Stn north exit), Karuizawa Town
places.notes: [GF] — A rare celiac-friendly soba house: 100% buckwheat with no wheat binder or dusting flour, gluten-free tsuyu, AND gluten-free (rice+buckwheat) tempura, so a 13-year-old celiac can have the full soba-and-tempura meal — better than the Nagano main branch. 2 min from Karuizawa station, 2F, 22 seats. On arrival, confirm in Japanese that the noodles are boiled in a dedicated pot / fresh water (専用の茹で湯) since that is the one detail not documented online.

chef_bio.background: 信州十割そば てん 軽井沢店 is a branch that opened in July 2025 on the 2F of the Ashizawa Building, a 2-minute walk from the north exit of JR Karuizawa station. It serves 十割そば milled from 100% Shinshu (Kijimadaira) buckwheat with no wheat binder, and was set up so that the whole gluten-free package — noodles, dipping tsuyu and tempura — is wheat-free, building on the main branch's gluten-free tsuyu. The menu leans on local Karuizawa produce (e.g. 軽井沢トマト).
chef_bio.philosophy: Serve honest 100% buckwheat soba that celiac and gluten-avoiding guests can eat in full, including the sauce and the tempura.
chef_bio.specialty: Jūwari seiro soba with a range of tsuyu (walnut, mozuku, grated radish, tororo, duck) and gluten-free rice-flour/buckwheat tempura.

anecdotes.text: Coverage highlights that the branch offers 「本店と同じグルテンフリーのつゆ」 plus gluten-free tempura and dishes using local ingredients such as 軽井沢トマト.
A Karuizawa food blogger reviewed it as 「グルテンフリーの美味しいお蕎麦屋さん」.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Web-Komachi and pikyoblog confirm a July-2025 Karuizawa-station branch of 信州十割そば てん serving 十割 (no-binder) soba, gluten-free tsuyu and gluten-free rice/buckwheat tempura; HotPepper/Tabelog give address, hours (11–15/17–21, closed Mon) and full menu with prices.

gf_cross_contamination.text: 十割そば 「つなぎを一切使わない」 with 打ち粉不使用 (no wheat binder, no wheat dusting flour); soba-only kitchen. Confirm a dedicated boiling pot on arrival — not documented online.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 「本店と同じグルテンフリーのつゆ」 — the dipping sauce is stated to be gluten-free (wheat-free), unusual for a soba shop.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Tsuyu is dashi-based (likely bonito); vegetable tempura batter is rice+buckwheat flour — confirm no egg/fish for vegan needs.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Whole GF offering (noodles, tsuyu, tempura) is a deliberate concept, so staff are geared toward gluten-free requests.

positives.text: 「米粉と蕎麦粉を合わせて揚げたグルテンフリーの天ぷら」— gluten-free tempura (rice + buckwheat flour, no wheat), letting celiacs have tempura with the soba.

places.name: Shinshu Juwari Soba Ten, Nagano Station Branch (信州十割そば てん 長野駅前店)
places.gf_detail: This shop serves ONLY 100% buckwheat soba — the venue's own copy reads 「長野県・木島平産そば粉100％で作る十割そば」 (jūwari soba made from 100% Kijimadaira-grown buckwheat flour) and 「打ち粉不使用」 (no dusting flour used). Tempura is also promoted as gluten-free: 「天ぷらもそば粉と米粉が衣に使用され、グルテンフリー」, fried in rice oil (米油). Because no wheat-flour soba is on the menu, shared-boiling-water contamination from wheat noodles is largely removed — but the key unresolved risk is the tsuyu (dipping/broth), which is normally wheat-based soy sauce and is NOT confirmed to be tamari/GF at THIS Nagano branch (only the Karuizawa branch publicly states 「そばつゆも完全グルテンフリー」). Treat as ask until the Nagano branch confirms a GF tsuyu.
places.vegan_detail: Soba noodles are 100% buckwheat (plant-based), and vegetable/mushroom tempura (e.g. 舞茸天) uses a buckwheat-and-rice-flour batter fried in rice oil, so some plant items exist. However, the standard soba tsuyu almost certainly contains katsuo (bonito) dashi, and no dedicated vegan menu or vegan-dashi option is documented. A vegan diner would need to confirm dashi and batter (egg) ingredients on-site.
places.neighborhood: 長野市南千歳 (Minami-Chitose, Nagano Stn Zenkoji-guchi / west exit)
places.notes: [GF] Strong base for celiac: this branch serves ONLY jūwari (100% buckwheat) soba with 打ち粉不使用 (no dusting flour) and no wheat noodles on the premises, so boiling-water cross-contact from wheat soba is minimal; tempura batter is buckwheat+rice flour fried in rice oil. The one make-or-break item is the tsuyu — confirm it is tamari-based / gluten-free at THIS Nagano branch (only Karuizawa publicly confirms GF tsuyu). Ask staff to use a clean pot / fresh boiling water and to serve a GF tsuyu; if they cannot confirm a wheat-free tsuyu, a 13-year-old celiac should skip the dipping broth and eat the soba plain or with GF tamari brought along.

chef_bio.philosophy: The shop's stated concept is casual, affordable jūwari soba made from 100% Kijimadaira (Nagano) buckwheat with no dusting flour, plus gluten-free tempura — soba the venue frames as approachable everyday dining near the station.
chef_bio.specialty: Jūwari (100% buckwheat) soba — choice of white 更科 (sarashina) or dark 挽きぐるみ (hikigurumi) — and gluten-free tempura.
chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 日本語の紹介記事（Web-Komachi、号外NET長野、ATRASHOME、芹田不動産ブログ）は店の特徴（木島平産そば粉100％の十割そば、打ち粉不使用、タブレット注文、2022年9月1日オープン、20席）を扱うが、店主・料理人個人の氏名や経歴には一切触れていない。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 打ち粉不使用 (no dusting flour) and the shop serves only 100% buckwheat jūwari soba — no wheat noodles on the menu — so shared-boiling-water contamination from wheat soba is largely eliminated.
Dedicated vs shared boiling water is not explicitly stated; still worth asking staff to boil in a clean pot / fresh water for a celiac guest.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Tsuyu (もりつゆ / くるみつゆ / とろろ) at the Nagano branch is not confirmed to be tamari/gluten-free; standard soba tsuyu uses wheat soy sauce. Only the separate Karuizawa branch publicly states 「天ぷら、そばつゆも完全グルテンフリー」.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Standard soba tsuyu likely contains bonito (katsuo) dashi; no vegan-dashi option documented.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No documented formal allergen protocol; ordering is via tablet. GF is a promoted feature (soba + tempura) but tsuyu handling for celiac must be confirmed with staff on-site.

positives.text: 「長野県・木島平産そば粉100％で作る十割そば」— only 100% buckwheat jūwari soba served, with 打ち粉不使用 (no dusting flour).
「天ぷらもそば粉と米粉が衣に使用され、グルテンフリー」— tempura batter is buckwheat + rice flour, fried in rice oil (米油).

places.name: Sorebana (そればな)
places.gf_detail: A small gluten-free baked-goods shop and tea room run solo by an owner who herself lives gluten-free. Her own sources state 「全商品グルテンフリー」 (all products gluten-free) and 「小麦粉・白砂糖を使わず」 (no wheat flour or white sugar), and she mills her own rice (Koshihikari for chewy dorayaki, Sasashigure for lighter bread). With a celiac owner who uses no wheat flour at all and mills her own rice, this is a wheat-free kitchen on the owner’s own word — rated high (only a single-source corroboration keeps it from dedicated). The practical catch is access, not safety: the shop opens only a few days a month on an irregular schedule and is takeout by advance phone reservation, so confirm the day and reserve before relying on it.
places.vegan_detail: White-sugar-free rice-flour bakes; some items (e.g. certain dorayaki fillings, fruit tarts) may be plant-based but others use butter, ham-and-cheese (Oldies but Goodies) or egg. Ask which of the day's items are dairy/egg free.
places.hours_raw: 12:00–17:00 (L.O. 16:00); open only a few days a month (irregular — check Instagram); takeout by advance phone reservation
places.neighborhood: 北深志1丁目（松本市中心部北）(Kitafukashi 1, north-central Matsumoto)
places.notes: [GF] Owner-is-celiac rice-flour bakery where 全商品グルテンフリー — one of the safest-sounding stops in Matsumoto for a celiac kid. Catch: it opens only a few days a month on an irregular schedule and takeout needs an advance phone call (0263-88-6718), so confirm the day before going. Everything is wheat- and white-sugar-free; still ask about any shared equipment.

chef_bio.background: Toshika Miyake opened Sorebana on 24 May 2020 in the Kitafukashi district, in a space formerly occupied by a bread shop called 'fiddle'. She follows a gluten-free lifestyle herself and began by making rice-flour bread and sweets at home before opening the shop, wanting to make things that taste good 「グルテンフリーをしている人にも、していない人にも」 (for people who eat gluten-free and those who don't alike). She uses organic unsprayed rice, selecting varieties by texture — Koshihikari for chewy dorayaki, Sasashigure for lighter bread — and mills most of it in-house. The name comes from abbreviating 「それていく話」, evoking the pleasure of rambling, meandering conversation. A café space was originally planned but postponed due to COVID-19; she runs the shop solo.
chef_bio.philosophy: All-gluten-free, white-sugar-free, body-kind rice-flour bakes that anyone can enjoy.
chef_bio.specialty: Dorayaki (plain/butter/mochi), rice-flour bread sandwiches, muffins, fruit tarts

anecdotes.text: The ham-and-cheese sandwich uses ham and cheese specially made by the Matsumoto shop Oldies but Goodies.
She chooses different rice varieties for different products — Koshihikari for a mochi-like dorayaki, Sasashigure for a lighter bread — and grinds most rice herself.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Matsumoto Keizai Shimbun (2020) profiles owner Toshika Miyake opening a gluten-free, white-sugar-free rice-flour bakery in Kitafukashi; her own site/STORES and the gf-akane blogger confirm 全商品グルテンフリー, the menu and advance-reservation takeout. Owner-detail well sourced → medium confidence.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Owner states all products are gluten-free (全商品グルテンフリー), uses no wheat flour, and mills her own rice — an effectively wheat-free kitchen; confirm no shared equipment.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Bakery/tea room; no soy-sauce savoury cooking noted.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Some bakes use butter, egg, or ham-and-cheese; not all items vegan.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Owner is herself gluten-free and built the shop for gluten-free customers; takeout requires an advance phone reservation.

positives.text: All products gluten-free and white-sugar-free; owner-celiac dedicated rice-flour bakery.

places.name: SuryaDev (スーリヤデーヴ)
places.gf_detail: Fully plant-based South Indian kitchen where the core of the meal (rice, sambar, rasam, dal, vegetable curries) is naturally wheat-free, but the restaurant itself does not run a gluten-free kitchen by default. Its own listing states 「グルテンフリー対応できます。事前にご連絡ください」 ("We can accommodate gluten-free; please contact us in advance"), so a celiac-safe plate must be arranged when you book, not on the day. Wheat is present in the kitchen — chapati/roti/naan on the rotating North Indian thali, papad, non-alcoholic beer (barley), and asafoetida (hing) commonly cut with wheat flour — so cross-contact is possible unless the advance-reserved GF version is prepared. Treat as GF-on-request, not dedicated.
places.vegan_detail: Entirely vegan (all-vegan) South Indian restaurant run in line with the adjacent ashram's philosophy: no meat, no fish, no dairy, and no five pungent roots — 五葷不使用, meaning no garlic or onion either. The venue describes itself plainly as 「オールヴィーガン」. Everything on the menu, including chai and lassi, is plant-based (soy milk).
places.hours_raw: By reservation only, seatings at 11:30 / 12:00 / 12:30 / 13:00 / 13:30 (reserve by 21:00 the day before); closed Thu & Fri. Check the business calendar.
places.neighborhood: 長野市七二会 (Shichinikai — rural, car needed)
places.notes: [GF] Reservation-only, rural (car needed) vegan South Indian near the Radha Krishna Ashram. Gluten-free is possible ONLY if requested when booking — the site says 「グルテンフリー対応できます。事前にご連絡ください」 — so state a celiac (not just 'no gluten') need in the reservation, ideally in writing/Japanese, and confirm no wheat, no papad, and wheat-free asafoetida. Naturally rice/lentil dishes (meals base, dosa, biryani) are the safest picks; avoid the North Indian thali (roti/naan) and the non-alcoholic beer (barley). Kitchen is 100% vegan, so no animal cross-contact — the only real risk to manage is wheat cross-contact.

chef_bio.background: The owner-chef is a practitioner at the adjacent Radha Krishna Ashram (ラダ・クリシュナ・アシュラム) in Shichinikai, rural Nagano, and opened SuryaDev in October 2021 in a renovated traditional farmhouse (kominka) with about 20 seats.
chef_bio.philosophy: Cooks to the ashram's sattvic principles: strictly plant-based, and free of the five pungent ingredients (garlic and onion excluded, 五葷不使用), using organic and 'oriental vegetable' produce.
chef_bio.specialty: Authentic South Indian meals (ミールス) served on a banana leaf, with occasional dosa, biryani and North Indian thali plates.

anecdotes.text: Diners eat looking out onto banana trees visible from the farmhouse windows; the restaurant is described as a 'satoyama' (rural-mountain) retreat along Route 19 near the Sasadaira tunnel.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 日本語ソース（vegewel、search-ethnic、コジータism、芹田不動産ブログ）によれば、オーナーは隣接するラダ・クリシュナ・アシュラムの修行者で、2021年10月に古民家を改装して開業。動物性・五葷を一切使わないオールヴィーガンの南インドミールスを予約制で提供。シェフの実名は公開情報になし。

gf_cross_contamination.text: GF is by advance request only — 「グルテンフリー対応できます。事前にご連絡ください」 — so the default meal is not prepared in a gluten-free workflow. Wheat items (chapati/roti/naan on North Indian thali, papad, barley non-alcoholic beer) and possibly wheat-containing asafoetida (hing) are used in the same small kitchen, so cross-contact is a real risk unless the reserved GF plate is made.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: South Indian cooking here uses no soy sauce; seasoning is via sambar/rasam spice bases, so wheat-in-soy-sauce is not a typical exposure. Not explicitly documented — confirm on reservation.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Kitchen is 100% vegan (all-vegan, no meat/fish/dairy/egg and no garlic or onion), so there is no animal-product cross-contact risk.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Reservation-only with English-speaking staff and an English menu; the venue explicitly offers gluten-free accommodation when notified in advance, indicating willingness to handle dietary requests.

positives.text: Fully vegan, no garlic/onion, largely rice- and lentil-based South Indian cuisine that is naturally low in wheat, plus a stated willingness to do gluten-free on advance notice — a good starting point for a celiac if arranged carefully.

places.name: TADACHIYA cafe THE GATE (タダチヤ カフェ ザ ゲート)
places.gf_detail: Nagano's first churro specialty café, inside the TADACHIYA cosmetics shop by Matsumoto Castle. The entire churro line is fried from a rice-flour dough — the venue states «長野県産の米粉を使った自家製のオリジナル生地» (homemade original dough using Nagano-grown rice flour), and its own allergen listing names only egg, rice flour and milk, with NO wheat.
