A wheat-allergy blogger describes it as «長野県産の米粉を使ったグルテンフリーのチュロス». Reality check: churros, soft-serve and coffee are the whole menu and none contain wheat, but the shop makes no formal celiac/gluten-free claim, states no dedicated fryer, and toppings/handling are unverified — treat as a genuinely wheat-free product with unconfirmed cross-contact, not a certified GF kitchen.
places.vegan_detail: The rice-flour churro dough contains egg and milk, and the sundaes are built on dairy soft-serve, so the sweets are not vegan. Black coffee (americano, iced coffee) is the only reliably plant-based option. One tourism blurb loosely mentioned a 'vegan' option, but the venue's own allergen list (egg/milk) contradicts this for the churros — confirm on site before assuming any vegan sweet.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–17:00, closed Wednesdays and year-end/New Year holidays
places.neighborhood: 大手 (Ōte), 松本城・四柱神社そば / near Matsumoto Castle & Yohashira Shrine, Matsumoto
places.notes: [GF] — Every churro here is fried from Nagano rice-flour dough and the posted allergens are only egg, rice flour and milk (no wheat), so the sweets themselves are wheat-free — unusually good for a Japanese café. But there is no dedicated-fryer statement and no celiac protocol, so for a celiac child confirm the fryer oil isn't shared with any wheat item and that toppings are wheat-free. Contains egg and dairy; not suitable for egg/milk allergy.

chef_bio.background: TADACHIYA cafe THE GATE is operated by 田立屋 (TADACHIYA), a long-standing Matsumoto cosmetics specialty retailer, and occupies the ground floor of its shop on Daimachi-dōri, the approach street to Matsumoto Castle. The café opened in 2025 as Nagano Prefecture's first dedicated churro specialty shop (「信州初！チュロス専門カフェ」/「長野県内初のチュロス専門店」). Owner Omiya Yasunao built the concept around a churro machine imported directly from Spain (「スペインから輸入した機械」) and an original dough made from Nagano-grown rice flour. The signature item, the Matsumoto Castle Sundae, layers black-and-white soft-serve to evoke the castle's donjon, topped with a fresh-fried churro.
chef_bio.philosophy: Showcase local Nagano rice flour in a freshly fried, made-to-order churro — crisp outside, chewy inside — as a castle-town sweet.
chef_bio.specialty: Rice-flour churros (7+ flavors) and the Matsumoto Castle soft-serve sundae

anecdotes.text: Each churro is roughly 35cm long and fried to order one at a time in a machine imported from Spain.
The Matsumoto Castle Sundae's black soft-serve is colored with bamboo charcoal and cocoa to mirror the castle's black-lacquer walls.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 田立屋 official café page confirms rice-flour dough, allergens (egg/rice flour/milk) and full price list; arura-media (2025-08) and web-komachi confirm owner-side story, Spanish machine and 'Nagano's first churro specialty shop'; visitmatsumoto (official tourism) confirms hours/location; a wheat-allergy blogger (gf-akane) lists it as a gluten-free rice-flour churro spot.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The full menu (rice-flour churros, soft-serve, coffee) contains no wheat item, so there is nothing wheat-based to contaminate the fryer — but the venue makes no dedicated-fryer or dedicated-utensil statement.
Wheat-allergy blogger lists it only as a researched GF rice-flour churro spot and did not visit or verify cross-contact herself.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce used — this is a sweets/coffee café with no savory soy-seasoned dishes.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Churro dough and sundaes contain egg and dairy; only black coffee is plant-based, so vegan cross-contact is a broad concern.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Venue posts a plain allergen list (egg, rice flour, milk) but publishes no allergy-handling or cross-contact protocol; ask staff directly.

positives.text: «長野県産の米粉を使った自家製のオリジナル生地» — dough is made from Nagano-grown rice flour, and the posted allergens are only egg, rice flour and milk, with no wheat listed.
Churros are made to order one at a time, so a fresh batch can be requested.

places.name: Temariya Shinshu-Matsumoto Baumkuchen Kobo (信州松本バウムクーヘン工房 てまりや)
places.gf_detail: A baumkuchen workshop-and-shop that markets itself as 「グルテンフリーバウムクーヘン専門店」 using 「信州産コシヒカリの米粉、玄米粉」 (Shinshu Koshihikari rice flour and brown-rice flour) at 100% instead of wheat. The Instagram handle and multiple listings label it gluten-free, but the shop's own concept page phrases the flour as 「米粉、玄米粉をはじめ」 (rice/brown-rice flour, among others) and carries no explicit 小麦不使用/専用設備 (wheat-free / dedicated-facility) statement. Because rice-flour and wheat baumkuchen can be baked on the same rollers, a celiac should confirm the specific product is 100% 米粉 and ask about shared equipment before buying — treat as retail GF options, not a guaranteed dedicated line.
places.vegan_detail: Baumkuchen is made with eggs (Shinshu 'noni' eggs) and butter, so the products are not vegan. No vegan baumkuchen line was found.
places.hours_raw: 10:00–17:00; closed 2nd & 4th Thursdays (irregular — check calendar)
places.neighborhood: 中央2丁目（松本市街地）(Chuo 2, central Matsumoto)
places.notes: [GF] Retail souvenir shop (no dining) selling rice-flour baumkuchen billed as gluten-free. Good for a travel gift or a treat, but it is a bake-shop, not a certified celiac facility — confirm the exact product is 100% 米粉 and ask about shared baking equipment. Products keep 2-3 weeks at room temperature.

chef_bio.background: Temariya is a Matsumoto baumkuchen workshop-and-shop specialising in rice-flour (米粉/玄米粉) cakes made with Shinshu Koshihikari rice flour and local eggs, hand-baked ring by ring. Its signature 「てまりん」 is a round baum with chestnut-and-bean filling shaped to evoke the local Matsumoto-temari craft ball, alongside varieties such as 「あるぷす」 (fermented butter + roasted brown-rice flour), 「あわゆき」, 「みのり」 and a charcoal-black 「まつもと」 inspired by Matsumoto Castle. No individual owner/founder biography is documented in the Japanese sources reviewed, so the profile is kept light.
chef_bio.philosophy: Gluten-free rice-flour baumkuchen showcasing Shinshu ingredients as a Matsumoto souvenir.
chef_bio.specialty: Rice-flour baumkuchen (Temarin, Alps, Awayuki, Minori, Matsumoto)

anecdotes.text: The Instagram account brands the shop as a gluten-free Shinshu-Matsumoto souvenir baumkuchen workshop.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Official site/shop and concept pages, Tabelog, Yahoo Map and zukulabo describe a rice-flour ('gluten-free') baumkuchen specialty shop in central Matsumoto (中央2-4-15) using 100% Shinshu rice/brown-rice flour, 10:00-17:00, irregular Thursday closures. No dedicated-facility or explicit wheat-free statement found; single-blogger GF-tour corroboration → confidence low.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Marketed as 100% rice-flour baumkuchen but no explicit wheat-free/dedicated-equipment statement found; confirm the specific product and shared-roller risk before buying.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Sweets retailer — no soy-sauce dishes.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Products contain egg and butter; not vegan.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Featured on a wheat-allergy blogger's Matsumoto GF tour as a rice-flour baumkuchen option.

positives.text: Signature line uses 100% Shinshu rice flour / brown-rice flour instead of wheat and is branded gluten-free.
